children.heading: Agency or instrumentality of a foreign state
children.label: 1 Agency or instrumentality of a foreign state
children.text: The term “agency or instrumentality of a foreign state” has the meaning given that term in .section 1603(b) of title 28

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 2 Appropriate congressional committees
children.text: The term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on International Relations and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate.

children.heading: Commercial activity
children.label: 3 Commercial activity
children.text: The term “commercial activity” has the meaning given that term in .section 1603(d) of title 28

children.label: i
children.text: without the property having been returned or adequate and effective compensation provided; or

children.label: ii
children.text: without the claim to the property having been settled pursuant to an international claims settlement agreement or other mutually accepted settlement procedure; and

children.label: i
children.text: a debt of any enterprise which has been nationalized, expropriated, or otherwise taken by the Cuban Government;

children.label: ii
children.text: a debt which is a charge on property nationalized, expropriated, or otherwise taken by the Cuban Government; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a debt which was incurred by the Cuban Government in satisfaction or settlement of a confiscated property claim.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “Cuban Government” includes the government of any political subdivision of Cuba, and any agency or instrumentality of the Government of Cuba.

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “agency or instrumentality of the Government of Cuba” means an agency or instrumentality of a foreign state as defined in , with each reference in such section to “a foreign state” deemed to be a reference to “Cuba”.section 1603(b) of title 28

children.heading: Democratically elected government in Cuba
children.label: 6 Democratically elected government in Cuba
children.text: The term “democratically elected government in Cuba” means a government determined by the President to have met the requirements of .section 6066 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the economic embargo (including all restrictions on trade or transactions with, and travel to or from, Cuba, and all restrictions on transactions in property in which Cuba or nationals of Cuba have an interest) that was imposed against Cuba pursuant to , , the Cuban Democracy Act of 1992 ( and following), or any other provision of law; andsection 2370(a) of this titlesection 4305(b) of title 5022 U.S.C. 6001

children.label: B
children.text: the restrictions imposed by section 902(c) of the Food Security Act of 1985.

children.label: A
children.text: an alien; or

children.label: B
children.text: any corporation, trust, partnership, or other juridical entity not organized under the laws of the United States, or of any State, the District of Columbia, or any commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Knowingly
children.label: 9 Knowingly
children.text: The term “knowingly” means with knowledge or having reason to know.

children.heading: Official of the Cuban Government or the ruling political party in Cuba
children.label: 10 Official of the Cuban Government or the ruling political party in Cuba
children.text: The term “official of the Cuban Government or the ruling political party in Cuba” refers to any member of the Council of Ministers, Council of State, central committee of the Communist Party of Cuba, or the Politburo of Cuba, or their equivalents.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 11 Person
children.text: The term “person” means any person or entity, including any agency or instrumentality of a foreign state.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “property” means any property (including patents, copyrights, trademarks, and any other form of intellectual property), whether real, personal, or mixed, and any present, future, or contingent right, security, or other interest therein, including any leasehold interest.

children.label: i
children.text: the claim to the property is held by a United States national and the claim has been certified under title V of the International Claims Settlement Act of 1949 [ et seq.]; or22 U.S.C. 1643

children.label: ii
children.text: the property is occupied by an official of the Cuban Government or the ruling political party in Cuba.

children.label: i
children.text: sells, transfers, distributes, dispenses, brokers, manages, or otherwise disposes of confiscated property, or purchases, leases, receives, possesses, obtains control of, manages, uses, or otherwise acquires or holds an interest in confiscated property,

children.label: ii
children.text: engages in a commercial activity using or otherwise benefiting from confiscated property, or

children.label: iii
children.text: causes, directs, participates in, or profits from, trafficking (as described in clause (i) or (ii)) by another person, or otherwise engages in trafficking (as described in clause (i) or (ii)) through another person,

children.label: i
children.text: the delivery of international telecommunication signals to Cuba;

children.label: ii
children.text: the trading or holding of securities publicly traded or held, unless the trading is with or by a person determined by the Secretary of the Treasury to be a specially designated national;

children.label: iii
children.text: transactions and uses of property incident to lawful travel to Cuba, to the extent that such transactions and uses of property are necessary to the conduct of such travel; or

children.label: iv
children.text: transactions and uses of property by a person who is both a citizen of Cuba and a resident of Cuba, and who is not an official of the Cuban Government or the ruling political party in Cuba.

children.heading: Transition government in Cuba
children.label: 14 Transition government in Cuba
children.text: The term “transition government in Cuba” means a government that the President determines is a transition government consistent with the requirements and factors set forth in .section 6065 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any United States citizen; or

children.label: B
children.text: any other legal entity which is organized under the laws of the United States, or of any State, the District of Columbia, or any commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States, and which has its principal place of business in the United States.
